report_name: Dutch Authorities Dismantle 'Asocks' Botnet Enslaving 17 Million Devices

threat_actors.name: Asocks Operators
threat_actors.description: The criminal enterprise operating the 'Asocks' residential proxy service and its associated botnet of 17 million devices. The group monetized the botnet by selling proxy access to other cybercriminals before their infrastructure was dismantled by Dutch authorities.
threat_actors.goals: Financial profit from selling illicit proxy access.

attack_patterns.name: Proxy
attack_patterns.description: The Asocks malware turned victim devices into proxy nodes, routing traffic for third parties to anonymize their malicious activities.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The Asocks proxyware malware was distributed by being bundled with free software and cracked applications.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Phishing was mentioned as a delivery vector for the Asocks proxyware malware.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The Asocks malware establishes a connection to its command and control server to register the device and await instructions, likely using standard web protocols.

malware.name: Asocks
malware.description: A proxyware malware that infects computers, mobile phones, and IoT devices, incorporating them into a massive botnet. The infected devices are used as residential proxies, with their bandwidth and IP addresses sold to cybercriminals.

campaigns.name: PROXYLIB
campaigns.description: A campaign mentioned in previous research that highlights Android devices as a key target for proxyware infection, likely contributing to the Asocks botnet.

identities.name: Dutch Politie
identities.description: The Dutch national police force, which led the international law enforcement operation to seize and dismantle the Asocks botnet infrastructure.

identities.name: National Cyber Security Center (NCSC)
identities.description: The Dutch National Cyber Security Center, which participated in the takedown of the Asocks botnet.

infrastructure.name: Asocks C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: A network of over 200 backend servers hosted in the Netherlands that served as the command and control infrastructure for the Asocks botnet. This infrastructure was seized and taken offline by Dutch authorities.

indicators.name: Potential Proxyware Process Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Proxyware Process Execution
id: 2b1a9c8d-4e3f-4b1a-8c7d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects process names commonly associated with proxy or tunneling software, which could indicate an infection by proxyware like Asocks.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/asocks-botnet-takedown.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1090
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|contains:
      - 'proxy'
      - 'tunnel'
  filter_known_good:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\procexp.exe'
      - '\procexp64.exe'
      - '\Fiddler.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_known_good
indicators.description: Detects the execution of processes with names commonly associated with proxy or tunneling software, based on hunting hints for detecting proxyware like Asocks.
